overall.description: A ship simulator game depicting a first-person view from the vessel's bridge during a severe nighttime storm. The player navigates through rough seas while monitoring radar and navigation displays, with occasional lightning strikes illuminating the dark ocean horizon.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigating through open ocean storm

events.description: lightning strike illuminates the horizon on the right side of the frame

agents.ego_description: navigating the ship through rough stormy seas while monitoring the radar and navigation systems on the bridge displays

entered.detail: chart plotter screen showing navigation map with yellow landmass
circular radar screen with rotating yellow sweep arm
blue data display screen with system information
metal safety railing at the front of the bridge
front deck of the vessel with red navigation lights
rough stormy water with visible wave motion
dark overcast sky covering the horizon

segments.scene_description: The segment presents a continuous first-person perspective from a ship's bridge during a severe nighttime storm. The vessel moves steadily through rough seas while three navigation monitors display radar and chart data at the top of the frame. Around the 10-second mark, a bright lightning strike briefly illuminates the right horizon, highlighting the turbulent ocean conditions before the scene returns to darkness.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot maintains a continuous first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as it navigates a dark, turbulent ocean during a severe nighttime storm. It begins with the vessel moving steadily through rough seas, with three navigation monitors displaying radar and chart data at the top of the frame. As the ship pushes forward into the darkness, a sudden, bright lightning strike flashes across the right horizon, momentarily revealing the violent state of the water. The scene then shifts back into deep shadow, returning the environment to a state of oppressive darkness as the vessel continues its journey.
